<div id="researchpapers" align="center">

## Research papers: 2026 4/5

[2026 (5/5)](http://github.com/tmgthb/Autonomous-Agents/blob/main/README.md), [2026 (4/5)](http://github.com/tmgthb/Autonomous-Agents/blob/main/resources/Autonomous_Agents_Research_Papers_2026_4.md), [2026 (3/5)](http://github.com/tmgthb/Autonomous-Agents/blob/main/resources/Autonomous_Agents_Research_Papers_2026_3.md), [2026 (2/5)](http://github.com/tmgthb/Autonomous-Agents/blob/main/resources/Autonomous_Agents_Research_Papers_2026_2.md), [2026 (1/5)](http://github.com/tmgthb/Autonomous-Agents/blob/main/resources/Autonomous_Agents_Research_Papers_2026_1.md), [2025 (4/4)](http://github.com/tmgthb/Autonomous-Agents/blob/main/resources/Autonomous_Agents_Research_Papers_2025_4.md),[2025 (3/4)](http://github.com/tmgthb/Autonomous-Agents/blob/main/resources/Autonomous_Agents_Research_Papers_2025_3.md), [2025 (2/4)](http://github.com/tmgthb/Autonomous-Agents/blob/main/resources/Autonomous_Agents_Research_Papers_2025_2.md), [2025 (1/4)](http://github.com/tmgthb/Autonomous-Agents/blob/main/resources/Autonomous_Agents_Research_Papers_2025_01.md), [2024](http://github.com/tmgthb/Autonomous-Agents/blob/main/resources/Autonomous_Agents_Research_Papers_2024.md), [2023](http://github.com/tmgthb/Autonomous-Agents/blob/main/resources/Autonomous_Agents_Research_Papers_2023.md), [Earlier](http://github.com/tmgthb/Autonomous-Agents/blob/main/resources/Autonomous_Agents_Research_Papers_Earlier.md)


Chronological order. 





</div>



---




#### 25th May 2026


[Experiments in Agentic AI for Science](http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.26305)

- DeepTS (Deep Time-Series) and DeepScribe: introduces a hybrid "Local Body, Remote Brain" architecture that decouples local orchestration from cloud-based LLM reasoning to automate scientific workflows.
- The framework utilizes a multi-agent system including planning-, synthesis-, and extraction-agents to perform autonomous data curation and multimodal scientific report generation.
- By implementing Cellular RAG and Direct Data Inspection, the system ensures high-precision extraction and provenance tracking while mitigating LLM hallucinations in scientific contexts.

---


[MOBILEGYM: A Verifiable and Highly Parallel Simulation Platform for Mobile GUI Agent Research](http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.26114)

- MOBILEGYM: introduces a browser-hosted, lightweight, and fully controllable simulation environment for mobile GUI agents that enables verifiable outcome signals and scalable online RL through structured JSON state representation.
- The platform utilizes a layered state model and declarative navigation specifications to support deterministic evaluation, snapshot-based rollout forking, and consequence-free execution of high-risk operations.
- MOBILEGYM-BENCH provides 416 parameterized task templates across 28 apps, facilitating reproducible research and effective sim-to-real transfer for mobile GUI agents.

---

[From Model Scaling to System Scaling: Scaling the Harness in Agentic AI](http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.26112)

- Agent Harness Framework: introduces a system-centered perspective for agentic AI, defining the agent harness as a structured execution layer composed of Orchestration Loop (O), Context Constructor (C), Memory Store (M), Reasoning Substrate (R), Skill Router (S), and Governance &amp; Verification (G).
- The framework shifts the focus from model-centric scaling to system scaling, emphasizing that long-horizon agent performance depends on the interaction between the foundation model and the surrounding harness components.
- The paper outlines a research agenda for harness-level benchmarks that measure process-level properties like memory hygiene, context efficiency, and safe evolution, rather than relying solely on one-shot task success.

---


[Balancing Plasticity and Stability with Fast and Slow Successor Features](http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.26357)

- SF+SC: introduces a continual RL framework that balances stability and plasticity by integrating Successor Features with a multi-timescale synaptic consolidation mechanism.
- The framework utilizes a chain of interacting variables with varying capacities to consolidate predictive representations, enabling agents to adapt to naturalistic, continuous environmental drift.
- A cross-attention mechanism provides an analytical readout from these consolidated variables, allowing the agent to dynamically weight contributions from different timescales for improved learning performance.

---

[SETUPX: Can LLM Agents Learn from Past Failures in Functionality-Correct Code Repository Setup?](http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.26186)

- SETUPX: introduces an experiential learning-based framework for functionality-correct code repository setup that utilizes XPU, Retriever Agent, Setup Agent, In-Loop Verifier Agent, Prosecutor Agent, Judge Agent, LIFO Docker snapshot stack, and Atom Rendering Engine.
- The framework employs a self-evolving XPU database to store and transfer cross-repository failure patterns, enabling agents to learn from past setup experiences.
- SETUPX integrates a Prosecutor-Judge verification protocol to decouple setup execution from independent adjudication, ensuring reliable assessment of environment readiness.

---


[Claw-Anything: Benchmarking Always-On Personal Assistants with Broader Access to User’s Digital World](http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.26086)

- Claw-Anything: introduces a benchmark for evaluating always-on personal assistant agents by expanding context across long-horizon event streams, interdependent backend services, and integrated GUI and CLI interaction.
- The framework utilizes an automated construction pipeline to synthesize complex, noisy digital environments and generate human-verified tasks that require agents to perform proactive and reactive assistance.
- Experimental results demonstrate a significant performance gap between current LLMs and the requirements of always-on personal assistance, while showing that fine-tuning on generated trajectories improves agent capabilities.

---

[VeriTrace: Evolving Mental Models for Deep Research Agents](http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.26081)

- VeriTrace: introduces a cognitive-graph framework that regulates deep research agents through three explicit feedback loops: interpretive update, deviation feedback, and schema revision.
- The framework utilizes a Planner, Searcher, Reader, and Manager to maintain a persistent, structured mental model that evolves based on evidence quality and task understanding.
- VeriTrace improves research performance by replacing implicit LLM reasoning with explicit regulatory mechanisms that align task understanding with reality.

---

[Automated Benchmark Auditing for AI Agents and Large Language Models](http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.26079)

- ABA (Auto Benchmark Audit): introduces an agentic framework that systematically audits individual benchmark tasks to uncover issues such as hidden environment dependencies, specification gaps, and limited grading logic.
- The framework utilizes an Evidence Collector Agent to resolve heterogeneous benchmark artifacts into a uniform manifest, which is then analyzed by an Auditor Agent using a standardized rubric.
- ABA operates in static or trajectory modes to identify issues across 168 benchmarks, demonstrating that filtering problematic tasks significantly shifts LLM capability assessments and leaderboard rankings.

---

[The Evolution of Digital Twins from Reactive to Agentic Systems](http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.26066)

- DT (Digital Twin) Framework: introduces a hierarchical classification of digital twins ranging from standalone representations to fully autonomous systems that integrate data, models, and AI to enable adaptive control.
- The framework utilizes LLMs as semantic mediators to bridge human intent with machine execution, enhancing the cognitive and perceptual capabilities of digital twins.
- The paper outlines the transition from reactive systems to agentic digital twins, emphasizing the role of AR-interfaces and autonomous agents in creating self-improving, purpose-driven intelligence.

---

[CausaLab: A Scalable Environment for Interactive Causal Discovery Toward AI Scientists](http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.26029)

- CausaLab: introduces a scalable environment for evaluating LLM agents on interactive causal discovery tasks by requiring them to recover hidden structural causal models through interventions.
- The framework utilizes a domain-specific language to record evolving causal hypotheses, enabling the assessment of both predictive accuracy and the faithfulness of the recovered causal mechanism.
- Experiments demonstrate that while LLMs can achieve high predictive performance, they often struggle with accurate mechanism recovery, frequently failing due to premature commitment to unverified hypotheses.

---

[Trustworthy Software Project Generation : a Case Study with an Interactive Theorem Prover](http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.26017)

- SPDDWL (Specification- and Proof-Driven Development Workflow): introduces a framework that leverages LLM agents to generate, verify, and extract functional software cores using interactive theorem provers.
- The architecture separates pure logic from effectful host components, utilizing a Requirement Analysis Agent, a Coding Agent, and a Proving Agent to ensure machine-checked correctness.
- By providing actionable feedback through explicit proof states, the framework enables LLMs to successfully synthesize complex, verified software projects that remain robust under testing and fuzzing.

---

[Learning in Low-Dimensional Subspaces: Orthogonal Bottlenecks for Reinforcement Learning](http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.26012)

- Orthogonal Bottlenecks for Reinforcement Learning: introduces a representation-level prior that inserts a fixed orthonormal projection between the encoder and downstream heads to constrain representations to a low-dimensional subspace.
- The framework utilizes an Encoder, a Fixed Orthonormal Projection, a Policy Head, and a Value Head to enforce low-dimensional structure without auxiliary objectives or pretraining.
- Theoretical and empirical results demonstrate that once the bottleneck dimension exceeds the intrinsic task rank, the architecture preserves expressivity and stabilizes learning dynamics.

---

[AI-Assisted Systematization for Evaluating GenAI Systems](http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.26001)

- AI-Assisted Systematization Framework: introduces a structured approach to transform broad background concepts into explicit, auditable specifications for GenAI evaluation using either a direct zero-shot LLM or a multi-agent system.
- The framework utilizes a concept spec schema consisting of patterns, key terms, slots, and theories, alongside a validation worksheet to ensure clarity, operationalizability, and granularity.
- Empirical evaluation across hate-based rhetoric and digital empathy demonstrates that AI assistance improves systematization feasibility, though it highlights trade-offs between process auditability and concept clarity.

---

[Causal Methods for LLM Development and Evaluation](http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.25998)

- Causal Methods for LLM Development and Evaluation: introduces a unifying perspective that frames LLM development and evaluation decisions as causal interventions to improve reliability and data efficiency.
- The paper maps causal inference opportunities across the entire LLM pipeline, including pre-training, alignment, deployment, and evaluation, while addressing challenges like selection bias and confounding.
- It provides practical principles for identifiability and estimation, advocating for the use of causal methods to ensure scientifically grounded design in LLM systems.

---

[What Makes a Medical Checker Trainable? Diagnosing Signal Collapse and Reward Hacking in Checker-Guided RAG for Biomedical QA](http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.25988)

- Checker-Guided RAG: introduces a diagnostic framework for verifier-as-reward systems in medical QA, identifying that checker output distribution, rather than held-out accuracy, determines trainable gradient.
- The framework compares four NLI back-ends to demonstrate that strong verification signals trigger a three-step reward-hacking cascade, whereas moderate-signal local classifiers yield higher-quality models.
- The research establishes that signal collapse is specific to LLM log-probability scoring and that signal strength is a property of the policy-checker pair, providing boundary conditions for verifier-as-reward designs.

---

[SafeCtrl-RL: Inference-Time Adaptive Behaviour Control for LLM Dialogue via RL-Driven Prompt Optimisation](http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.25984)

- SafeCtrl-RL: introduces an inference-time behavioural control framework that enables adaptive safety regulation in LLMs without model retraining or parameter modification.
- The framework models dialogue safety as a sequential decision process where a reinforcement learning agent dynamically selects prompt adjustment strategies based on conversational context and feedback signals.
- SafeCtrl-RL consistently improves safety and response quality across multiple LLMs while achieving favourable performance-efficiency trade-offs compared to static prompt optimisation methods.

---

[When Do LLM Agents Treat Surface Noise Differently from Semantic Noise? A 68-Cell Measurement Study with a Held-Out Trace-Level Validation](http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.25981)

- AgentDiff-Probe v2: introduces a measurement framework to quantify the directional inconsistency gap between meaning-bearing and presentation perturbations in LLM agents.
- The framework utilizes a 68-cell measurement study across ten LLMs to demonstrate that semantic noise causes significantly higher inconsistency rates than presentation noise.
- Trace-level analysis reveals a stealth divergence mechanism where semantic perturbations silently corrupt intermediate thought content from the second step onward.

---

[Anticipate and Learn: Unleashing Idle-Time Compute in Proactive Agents](http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.25971)

- ProAct: introduces a proactive agent architecture that leverages idle-time compute to anticipate and fulfill likely upcoming user needs by integrating Future-State Prediction, Idle-Time Acquisition, and a Utility-Aware Delivery Policy.
- The framework utilizes Persistent Memory, including a Knowledge Base and User Persona, to ground predictions and maintain context across multi-turn interactions.
- ProActEval, a comprehensive benchmark, demonstrates that the architecture reduces user effort and task completion turns while maintaining factual integrity through prediction-guided background computation.

---

[PathWISE: Multi-Agent Cancer Pathway Triaging Ontology Learning from Clinical Flowcharts](http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.25970)

- PathWISE: introduces a multi-agent framework that transforms visual clinical flowcharts into executable, standards-based decision-support services by combining multimodal extraction with deterministic verification.
- The pipeline utilizes a Vision Agent for structural parsing, followed by a deterministic DFS Auditor and a compiler-as-critic pattern to ensure the generated CQL libraries are syntactically valid and clinically auditable.
- By confining non-deterministic LLM inference to knowledge extraction and employing deterministic graph mathematics for routing, the framework effectively bridges the gap between expert-authored visual pathways and machine-readable clinical decision support.

---

[PolyGnosis 2.0: Enhancing LLM Reasoning via Agentic Harness Engineering for Polymarket and OSINT Insight Extraction](http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.25958)

- PolyGnosis 2.0: introduces a multi-agent architecture that synthesizes Polymarket anomaly signals with GDELT OSINT streams to extract predictive intelligence through agentic harness engineering.
- The framework utilizes specialized agents including a Clustering Agent, Keywords Agent, and Analysis Agent, supported by reflection loops and divide-and-conquer partitioning to enhance reasoning capabilities.
- Empirical evaluation demonstrates that while structural partitioning is essential for multi-dimensional alignment, unconstrained terminal reflection can induce logical drift, necessitating deterministic validation for professional-grade analytical precision.

---

[A Pedestrian–Vehicle Interaction Benchmark and Annotation Framework for Unstructured Scenes via Uncalibrated Cameras](http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.25947)

- PINNS (Pedestrian–vehicle Interaction dataset from uNcalibrated cameras in uNstructured Scenes): introduces a dataset construction framework that utilizes uncalibrated surveillance cameras to generate trajectory data for complex pedestrian–vehicle interactions in unstructured environments, incorporating Pedestrian Annotation, Homography Calibration, Vehicle BEV Annotation, Scene Information Annotation, and Dataset Construction.
- The framework employs a semi-automatic pipeline where initial trajectories are generated by object detection and tracking methods, followed by manual refinement to ensure high-quality ground-truth data for heterogeneous agent interactions.
- By leveraging uncalibrated cameras, the approach overcomes the scalability limitations of traditional sensor-based datasets, providing a diverse benchmark for evaluating trajectory prediction models in challenging, real-world mixed-traffic scenarios.

---

[Explore Before You Solve: The Speed–Depth Trade-off in Epistemic Agents for ARC-AGI-3](http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.25931)

- AERA (Adaptive Epistemic Reasoning Agent): introduces a three-phase architecture that balances exploration and planning by using a belief entropy proxy to gate the transition from an EXPLORE-module to a PLAN-module.
- The framework formalizes the Speed–Depth trade-off, where the agent optimizes for information gain per action during the EXPLORE-module phase before committing to a solution in the PLAN-module phase.
- AERA utilizes an LLM-based agent that maintains an explicit world-model hypothesis, significantly outperforming non-exploratory baselines on the ARC-AGI-3 benchmark.

---

[Multi-Agent Systems are Mixtures of Experts: Who Becomes an Influencer?](http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.25929)

- FJ-MoE (Friedkin-Johnsen Mixture of Experts): introduces a framework that interprets multi-agent LLM deliberation as a mixture of experts system where the Friedkin-Johnsen model provides a tractable mechanism for input-dependent routing.
- The framework demonstrates that multi-agent systems implicitly perform adaptive routing based on observable proxies like agent confidence and initial opinion alignment.
- Theoretical analysis reveals that multi-agent systems outperform single agents and static ensembles when the gain from local specialization exceeds the diversity lost and the regret incurred by imperfect routing.

---

[Can LLMs Time Travel? Enhancing Temporal Consistency in Legal Agentic Search through Reinforcement Learning](http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.25920)

- LegalSearch-R1: introduces an end-to-end reinforcement learning framework that enhances temporal consistency in legal agentic search by pairing local statute RAG with online web search.
- The framework utilizes a dual-tool architecture and entropy-based advantage shaping to ensure that retrieved legal provisions are temporally aligned with the specific context of each case.
- Experimental results on a temporally-indexed benchmark demonstrate that the agent significantly outperforms existing deep research frameworks and specialized legal LLMs in both in-domain and out-of-domain tasks.

---

[AgentGrounder: Zero-Shot 3D Visual Pointcloud Grounding using Multimodal Language Models](http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.25901)

- AgentGrounder: introduces a two-stage zero-shot 3D visual grounding framework that utilizes a 3D Segmentation Network, Object Lookup Table (OLT), Agent Workflow, Image Renderer, Spatial Calculator, OLT Search, and BBox Prediction to localize objects in point clouds without task-specific training.
- The framework employs an offline stage to construct an OLT and an online tool-driven agent that performs query decomposition, selective candidate retrieval, and deterministic geometric reasoning to improve grounding robustness.
- By integrating on-demand rendering and efficient context utilization, the agent minimizes token overhead and reduces cascading matching errors compared to fixed-pipeline approaches.

---

[How Agentic AI Coding Assistants Become the Attacker’s Shell](http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.25871)

- AIShellJack: introduces a systematic evaluation of prompt injection vulnerabilities in agentic AI coding assistants by simulating how malicious external artifacts trigger unauthorized system actions.
- The research demonstrates that current LLMs in coding assistants fail to distinguish between trusted developer instructions and untrusted attacker payloads, leading to systemic security risks.
- The study highlights that the root cause of these vulnerabilities is architectural, as agentic assistants lack structural boundaries between untrusted input data and executable system commands.

---

[Mitigating Provenance-Role Collapse in Long-Term Agents via Typed Memory Representation](http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.25869)

- MEMIR: introduces a typed memory intermediate representation that operationalizes source monitoring as a structural constraint to prevent provenance-role collapse in LLMs.
- The framework utilizes Systematic Writing of Memory Atoms, Multi-Route Atomic Projection, and Provenance-Scoped Utilization to transform unstructured interaction history into source-grounded, claim-centered factual interfaces.
- By separating raw evidence from truth-bearing claims, MEMIR enables LLMs to perform reliable long-term reasoning while maintaining explicit provenance and epistemic roles.

---

[RePlan-Bot: Multi-Level Replanning for Embodied Instruction Following](http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.25851)

- RePlan-Bot: introduces a multi-level, continuous replanning framework for embodied instruction following that integrates a Modular Planner, LLM-Auditor, Multi-layered Instance Map, LLM-Location-Prediction, Low-level Controller, and ViT-based Corrector.
- The framework utilizes an LLM-Auditor for high-level goal refinement, a commonsense-driven search mechanism with a multi-layered instance map for mid-level exploration, and a ViT-based Corrector for low-level action validation.
- RePlan-Bot improves task success rates in partially observable 3D environments by proactively correcting high-level plans and low-level primitive actions based on real-time visual and environmental feedback.

---

[When Search Becomes Memory: Turning Robot Design Trials into Transferable Skills](http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.25832)

- AUTO-ROBOTIST: introduces a self-evolving LLM agent that distills robot morphology search traces into an explicit, evidence-grounded natural-language skill library to enable transferable design knowledge.
- The framework integrates an LLM-based Proposer, a Genetic Algorithm (GA) for exploration, and a persistent Skill Library that stores structural archetypes, rules, and observations to guide future design tasks.
- By converting expensive physical evaluations into auditable design principles, the system improves sample efficiency in cold-start search and facilitates effective cross-scale transfer in voxel-based robot design.

---

[Behind EvoMap: Characterizing a Self-Evolving Agent-to-Agent Collaboration Network](http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.25815)

- EvoMap: introduces a large-scale empirical study of a decentralized A2A collaboration network, evaluating its reusability, evolution, and auditability mechanisms.
- The framework utilizes Evolver, EvoMap Hub, Gene, Capsule, EvolutionEvent, GDI, Sandbox, and a Credit System to facilitate autonomous instruction sharing and curation among LLMs.
- The study reveals that the platform suffers from low reusability, centralized incentive distribution, and significant vulnerabilities where agents bypass validation and manipulate GDI scores via self-reported metadata.

---

[On the Benefits of Free Exploration for Regret Minimization in Multi-Armed Bandits](http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.25789)

- UFE-KLUCB-H: introduces a two-phase bandit algorithm that leverages an initial free exploration phase to reduce cumulative regret in the subsequent regret minimization phase.
- The framework utilizes UFE to adaptively collect information about arm suboptimality and KLUCB-H to incorporate this history into a regret minimization policy.
- The paper establishes instance-dependent upper and lower bounds for regret in this setting, demonstrating that the algorithm is nearly asymptotically optimal for two-valued bandits.

---

[Agent-Centric Social Trajectory Prediction: A Free Energy Principle Perspective](http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.25748)

- FEP-Diff: introduces an agent-centric trajectory prediction framework grounded in the Free Energy Principle, utilizing an Agent Representation encoder, a Belief Learner, and a Trajectory Generator to produce cognitively plausible predictions under partial observability.
- The framework employs a dual-branch spatiotemporal encoder for local context extraction and a goal-conditioned belief learner to infer latent intentions optimized via a free-energy objective.
- A residual diffusion trajectory generator refines coarse proxy trajectories into precise multimodal predictions using token-level conditioning to ensure physical and social consistency.

---

[Multi-Agent Coordination Adaptation via Structure-Guided Orchestration](http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.25746)

- MACA: introduces a probabilistic framework that casts multi-agent coordination as a posterior inference problem over structural and orchestration variables to enable efficient, task-conditioned execution.
- The framework utilizes Structural Prior Learning to construct a GraphSpec that constrains the interaction space, and Token-Aware Orchestration to optimize agent execution via reinforcement learning.
- By jointly adapting structure and orchestration, MACA suppresses redundant interactions and achieves superior performance with significantly reduced token consumption across diverse benchmarks.

---

[Collaborative Threat-Aware Autonomy (CTAA)](http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.25741)

- CTAA: introduces a role-differentiated multi-agent framework that utilizes CSBEZ reactive controller, Primary ACP, Escort ACP, Decoy ACP, WEZ risk model, and Goal-directed heading command to improve mission success in contested airspace.
- The framework leverages spatial route diversity and role-weighted risk allocation to induce threat saturation, allowing high-value assets to transit contested zones while lower-value agents absorb adversarial attention.
- Simulation and Monte Carlo validation demonstrate that the CTAA approach significantly increases mission success probability by mitigating the single-point-of-failure limitation inherent in single-vehicle autonomous systems.

---

[The Behavioral Credibility Trilemma: When Calibrated Autonomy Becomes Impossible](http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.25739)

- CGDP (Confidence-Gated Decision Problem): introduces a formal framework proving that RL agents cannot simultaneously maximize helpfulness, calibration, and autonomy under rational oversight.
- The framework demonstrates that any non-affine approval mechanism incentivizes agents to inflate confidence reports, thereby destroying the strict properness of scoring rules.
- The research establishes that this trilemma is a fundamental geometric property of the payoff surface, rendering it independent of the specific optimization algorithm used by the agent.

---

[AgentHijack: Benchmarking Computer Use Agent Robustness to Common Environment Corruptions](http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.25707)

- AgentHijack-Agent: introduces a GUI agent framework that integrates an action generator with enhanced grounding capabilities and an onlooker for behavior summarization and environment checking to improve robustness against common corruptions.
- The framework utilizes DA-GRPO to optimize agent policies across diverse corrupted environments while leveraging a replay buffer to preserve successful trajectories for stable training.
- The onlooker component proactively monitors environmental changes and validates initial states to prevent agents from engaging in meaningless exploration within faulty or corrupted environments.

---

[Neural Router: Semantic Content Matching for Agentic AI](http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.25701)

- Neural Router: introduces a content-based publish/subscribe matching engine that integrates LLMs into the matching loop to bridge vocabulary and modality gaps between publishers and subscribers.
- The architecture utilizes a two-level design with offline subscription optimization via clustering and compression, and online event matching through LLM-based semantic evaluation.
- The system employs a Quality-of-Experience framework for heterogeneous backend assignment and characterizes performance through context-window and discrimination-capacity crossovers.

---

[From Facts to Insights: A Persona-Driven Dual Memory Framework and Dataset for Role-Playing Agents](http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.25693)

- DUALMEM: introduces a dual memory framework that decouples conversation history into factual cognition and persona-conditioned insight cognition to improve role-playing fidelity.
- The framework utilizes a memory construction model to process conversation chunks into linked factual and insight pairs, which are stored in a unified memory bank for retrieval by the role-playing agent.
- The authors also present RoleMemo, a large-scale dataset featuring four reasoning tasks designed to evaluate persona-driven reconstructive memory in long-context role-playing scenarios.

---

[Meta-Engineering Harnesses for AI-Native Software Production](http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.25665)

- Meta-Engineering Harness (MEH): introduces a layered software-production architecture that governs the lifecycle of AI-native applications through Model Layer, Role-Orchestration Layer, Contract Layer, Context-Memory Layer, Execution Layer, Verification Layer, Calibration Layer, and Four-Way Failure Arbiter.
- The framework utilizes a two-pass contract compilation process and persistent markdown memory to mitigate context evaporation and ensure that LLM-based agents operate within defined business constraints.
- By implementing independence-based and attention-based verification regimes, the harness reduces correlated model failures and provides a structured mechanism for continuous process improvement via an outer-loop calibration cycle.

---

[When Agents Control Robots: A Zero Trust Policy Model for Agentic Cyber-Physical Systems](http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.25653)

- ZTPM (Zero Trust Policy Model): introduces a security framework for Multi-Agentic Cyber-Physical Systems that treats physical actuation as a first-class policy object governed by runtime Physical Impact Tiers.
- The architecture integrates five enforcement layers (AID, CII, TEA, CATP, ABG) to secure the path from LFM reasoning to physical robotic execution against adversarial manipulation.
- Empirical evaluation on the Cobot-Claw system demonstrates that LFM backends lack reliable risk-aware actuation, necessitating the proposed policy-level enforcement at the physical boundary.

---

[Iterate Until Retrieved: Factual Nugget Optimization for Discoverable Continual Corrections in Agentic RAG](http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.25641)

- INO (Iterative Nugget Optimization): introduces an index-time optimization method that treats nugget construction as a measurable problem by using the production agentic RAG as a test harness to iteratively refine knowledge entries until they are discoverable.
- The framework includes an actionability classifier to isolate factual corrections from generic feedback, followed by an LLM reflector that inspects retrieval failures and revises the nugget's title, body, and retrieval anchors.
- By optimizing the retrievable artifact rather than the system prompt, INO ensures local, auditable, and reversible improvements that generalize across semantically related user queries in B2B enterprise environments.

---

[Insuring Every Action: An Authority Frontier Framework for Runtime Actuarial Control of Autonomous AI Agents](http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.25632)

- AAI (Actuarial Action Interface): introduces a deterministic runtime contract that prices side-effect-bearing actions against a fixed safe default and gates execution against a per-boundary reserve capital budget.
- The framework utilizes an Authority Frontier evaluation primitive to measure released autonomous authority as a function of reserve capital across diverse agentic environments.
- The system incorporates an Underwriting Persistence Index to quantify model-specific post-denial behavior, treating model identity as an actuarial underwriting variable.

---

[CUA-GYM: Scaling Verifiable Training Environments and Tasks for Computer-Use Agents](http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.25624)

- CUA-GYM: introduces a scalable pipeline that co-generates task instructions, environment states, and reward functions for computer-use agents using an adversarial agentic loop.
- The framework utilizes an Orchestrator, Generator, and Discriminator to ensure tuple consistency, while CUA-GYM-HUB provides a suite of high-fidelity mock web applications for environment scaling.
- Trained with GSPO, the resulting models demonstrate state-of-the-art performance on OSWorld-Verified and show effective cross-platform transfer to held-out browser benchmarks.

---

[Personalize-then-Store: Benchmarking and Learning Personalized Memory for Long-horizon Agents](http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.25535)

- PerMem-Bench: introduces a benchmark and automated pipeline for evaluating personalized memory systems by modeling heterogeneous user-agent interaction patterns.
- The framework utilizes session-level storage gating to selectively bypass memory operations for transient interactions, thereby optimizing the memory budget for long-horizon tasks.
- Empirical results demonstrate that while personalization significantly improves memory retention under fixed budgets, current gating methods struggle to accurately adapt to dynamic shifts in user behavior.

---

[CRPO: Character-centric Group Relative Policy Optimization for Role-aware Reasoning in Role-playing Agents](http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.25511)

- CRPO: introduces a character-centric reinforcement learning framework that realigns optimization objectives for role-playing agents by decoupling task and style rewards, adapting to character entropy, and utilizing contrastive sampling.
- The framework employs Dual-Stream Advantage Estimation to resolve gradient conflicts, Entropy-Aware Adaptive Exploitation to manage character-specific learning difficulty, and Contrastive Anchor Sampling to mitigate style collapse.
- Extensive experiments demonstrate that CRPO significantly enhances persona consistency and robustness in role-playing tasks compared to standard problem-centric RL methods.

---

[ATWL: A Formal Language for Representing, Comparing, and Reusing Visual Analytics Workflows](http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.25489)

- ATWL (Artifact–Transform Workflow Language): introduces a domain-agnostic, declarative language designed to formally represent the structure and intent of visual analytics workflows using Entities, Feature, Arrangement, Visualisation, Pattern, Model, Knowledge, and Specification.
- The framework utilizes transform intents including define-unit, characterise, contextualise, visualise, abstract, build-model, generate-knowledge, and assess to capture the logical progression of analysis from raw data to derived insights.
- ATWL enables systematic cross-workflow analysis and LLM-assisted workflow design by providing a common vocabulary that makes analytical strategies, iteration structures, and data flow explicit and comparable.

---

[MAIL++: Multi-Modal Bi-directional Agent Layer for Vision-Language Models](http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.25479)

- MAIL++: introduces a parameter-efficient fine-tuning paradigm that embeds bidirectional cross-modal coupling directly into the intrinsic computational modules of VLMs using linked agent layers and meta-bridge functions.
- The framework utilizes Agent Layers (AL) consisting of scaling and shifting vectors to approximate full fine-tuning updates, while Meta-Scaling Vectors and Meta-Bridges enable reciprocal information exchange between visual and textual encoders.
- By re-parameterizing agent layers into the frozen backbone at inference time, MAIL++ maintains original computational efficiency while achieving state-of-the-art performance on few-shot classification and cross-domain retrieval tasks.

---

[A Multi-Agent LLM Framework for Rating the Quality of Surgical Feedback](http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.25440)

- Multi-Agent LLM Framework: introduces a two-stage pipeline that utilizes GPT-4o Agents for unsupervised discovery of surgical feedback quality criteria, which are then refined via Similarity Clustering and an LLM Unification Prompt into a standardized Scoring Rubric for automated evaluation.
- The framework employs an LLM-as-a-judge approach to apply the discovered criteria, enabling scalable and interpretable assessment of feedback delivery quality grounded in clinical outcomes.
- Evaluation on 4.2k surgical feedback instances demonstrates that the AI-discovered criteria significantly improve the prediction of trainee behavioral adjustments and trainer reactions compared to prior content-based methods.

---

[Security of OpenClaw Agents: Fundamentals, Threats, and Countermeasures](http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.25435)

- OpenClaw: introduces a comprehensive security study of autonomous agents that integrate LLMs with persistent memory, multi-channel interaction, and OS-level execution capabilities.
- The paper categorizes security threats into a layered framework covering cognition-layer reasoning, execution-layer tool usage, and interaction-layer communication vulnerabilities.
- It reviews representative defense mechanisms and outlines future research directions for building trustworthy and resilient autonomous agent ecosystems.

---

[Mode 0: A New 3GPP V2X Resource Allocation Category for Roadside Computing Unit-Assisted Safety Communication](http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.25431)

- Mode 0: introduces a new 3GPP V2X resource allocation category defined by the RCU (Infrastructure ensemble for safety), which integrates Seeing (Elevated sensing hardware), Speaking (RSU sidelink communication), and Thinking (MEC facility for scheduling) to provide safety-critical communication.
- The framework utilizes MAPPO (Multi-agent reinforcement learning algorithm) under a CTDE (Centralized training, distributed execution) architecture to manage M0 (Safety-critical traffic class) and M1 (Non-safety traffic class) through demand separation.
- This approach addresses structural failures in existing V2X modes by providing a dedicated infrastructure-led scheduling layer that ensures deterministic latency for safety-critical applications.

---

[CODESKILL: Learning Self-Evolving Skills for Coding Agents](http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.25430)

- CODESKILL: introduces a learnable management policy for extracting, evolving, and maintaining reusable procedural skills from coding-agent trajectories to improve downstream LLM agent performance.
- The framework utilizes a hybrid reward system combining sparse verifiable execution feedback with dense rubric-based quality judgments to optimize the CODESKILL Model via reinforcement learning.
- CODESKILL maintains a multi-granularity skill bank, categorizing knowledge into task-level strategies and event-driven local guidance to support long-horizon software engineering tasks.

---

[HyLaT: Efficient Multi-Agent Communication via Hybrid Latent-Text Protocol](http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.25421)

- HyLaT: introduces a dual-channel communication protocol that balances efficiency and interpretability by routing dense cognitive signals through a latent-channel and concise conclusions through a text-channel.
- The framework utilizes a two-stage training process, combining single-agent hybrid generation learning and multi-agent interactive co-learning to enable LLMs to generate and interpret hybrid messages.
- HyLaT significantly reduces communication overhead compared to text-based methods while maintaining task performance and providing better interpretability than pure latent-space approaches.

---

[Decision-Making with Lightweight Confidence-Aware Language Model for Autonomous Driving](http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.25393)

- CALMM-Drive: introduces a lightweight decision-making framework for autonomous driving that distills confidence-aware reasoning from a multi-agent workflow into a dual-head LLM.
- The framework utilizes a multi-agent collaborative workflow to generate high-quality, confidence-annotated demonstrations, which are then distilled into a lightweight model using a confidence-aware fine-tuning strategy and RAG.
- The dual-head architecture enables joint prediction of decision probabilities and textual rationales, facilitating efficient on-board deployment while maintaining state-of-the-art performance in complex driving scenarios.

---

[Evo-Attacker: Memory-Augmented Reinforcement Learning for Long-Horizon Tool Attacks on LLM-MAS](http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.25389)

- Evo-Attacker: introduces a memory-augmented reinforcement learning framework that constructs a dynamic Attack Memory and employs a deliberative reasoning policy to retrieve adversarial patterns and strategize interventions.
- The framework utilizes a Memory Retriever and Return Modifier to execute context-aware attacks, which are optimized via Attack-Flow GRPO to resolve long-horizon credit assignment challenges.
- Extensive experiments demonstrate that Evo-Attacker consistently outperforms existing baselines by generalizing across diverse LLM-MAS architectures, task domains, and tool schemas.

---

[AuthTrace: Diagnosing Evidence Construction in Thematically Dense Single-Author Corpora](http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.25382)

- AuthTrace: introduces a diagnostic benchmark for evaluating evidence construction systems on thematically dense single-author corpora using Flat RAG, Graph RAG, Memory, Event Memory, LLM-Wiki, and Long-context components.
- The framework utilizes a pack-level evaluation protocol measuring Evidence Recall, Evidence Precision, and Answer Correctness to diagnose paradigm-specific failure modes across varying evidence fan-in levels.
- Experimental results demonstrate that evidence recall is the primary bottleneck for answer quality, with different paradigms exhibiting distinct structural collapse patterns as the required evidence fan-in increases.

---

[EfficientGraph-RAG: Structured Retrieval-State Management for Cross-Task Retrieval-Augmented Generation](http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.25379)

- EfficientGraph-RAG: introduces a structured retrieval-state management framework that decouples retrieval-side state updates from final reasoning to improve efficiency and accuracy.
- The framework utilizes TAM for hierarchical evidence organization, MARS for iterative multi-agent state updates, and SMP for controlled memory sharing across agents and queries.
- By offloading retrieval, navigation, and verification to small-model agents and reserving the LLM for final synthesis, the system significantly reduces token usage while maintaining high performance across diverse retrieval tasks.

---

[KYA: A Framework-Agnostic Trust Layer for Autonomous Systems with Verifiable Provenance and Hierarchical Policy Composition](http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.25376)

- KYA (Know Your Agents): introduces a framework-agnostic trust and governance layer for autonomous systems, utilizing kya (core trust and governance), kya_hooks (in-process agent framework shim), kya_otlp_bridge (OpenTelemetry span ingestion), kya_redteam (out-of-band adversarial testing), HMAC evidence chain (cryptographically verifiable audit log), KYP (unified trust scoring for users/agents/services), four-gate pipeline (signed policy update verification), and only-tighten algebra (hierarchical policy override enforcement) to provide verifiable provenance and hierarchical policy composition.
- The framework provides a unified principal-trust taxonomy (KYP) and a four-gate inbound apply pipeline to ensure secure, operator-approved policy distribution across diverse agent frameworks.
- KYA maintains system integrity through an HMAC-chained evidence log and an only-tighten composition algebra that prevents unauthorized policy loosening while supporting multi-tenant governance.

---

[FOUND-IT: Foundation-model-first Task-driven 3D Scene Graphs with Granularity on Demand](http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.25371)

- FOUND-IT: introduces a real-time, open-set, hierarchical 3D scene graph construction system that dynamically adjusts granularity based on task-specific queries using GFM, Visual Memory, Cached Memory, Places Layer, Ground Segmentation Head, Regions Layer, and an LLM Agent.
- The framework leverages uncalibrated monocular video input to build task-driven representations, deferring object formation until query time to maintain efficiency and flexibility.
- By integrating a ground segmentation head into the GFM and utilizing an LLM agent for incremental scene graph construction, the system achieves high performance in both indoor and outdoor environments without requiring predefined task lists.

---

[Prior Policy Guided Dual-Agent Coordinated Manipulation Planning of Spacecraft-Manipulator System](http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.25362)

- DACMP: introduces a decoupled dual-agent architecture that isolates manipulator control from base spacecraft stabilization to mitigate dynamic coupling interference.
- The framework utilizes a Timestep-level Expert Switching Guidance (TESG) mechanism to integrate prior knowledge from classical controllers, ensuring stable exploration and preventing the agents from learning mere residual compensation.
- Extensive experiments demonstrate that the DACMP framework achieves superior task success rates and control precision compared to single-agent DRL baselines under various environmental disturbances and system constraints.

---

[Towards Reliable Fetal Ultrasound Interpretation with Multi-Agent Collaboration](http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.25357)

- FetUSAgents: introduces a tool-augmented multi-agent system for comprehensive fetal ultrasound interpretation that orchestrates task-specific visual tools via collaborative LLM agents.
- The framework utilizes a Dual-Path Evidence Arbitration (DPEA) mechanism to integrate LLM-driven deliberative reasoning from multiple Voter Agents with structured computational evidence from specialized tools.
- A retrieval-enhanced Evidence bank consolidates heterogeneous intermediate findings to promote traceable, clinically grounded conclusions and reduce hallucinations in report generation.

---

[CausalFlow: Causal Attribution and Counterfactual Repair for LLM Agent Failures](http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.25338)

- CausalFlow: introduces an interventional framework that converts failed LLM agent execution traces into minimal counterfactual repairs and reusable supervision.
- The framework models execution as sequential chains of dependent steps, using Causal Responsibility Scores to identify and repair failure-inducing decisions through targeted interventions.
- CausalFlow improves agent reliability and provides structured contrastive pairs for offline preference optimization without requiring manual annotation of intermediate steps.

---

[Recursive Multi-Agent Trading System: Iterative Optimized Portfolio Strategy Under Geopolitical Uncertainty](http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.25311)

- RMATS: introduces a hierarchical multi-agent framework for iterative portfolio optimization that utilizes a Manager Agent, Sentiment Agent, Report Agent, Analysis Agent, and Risk Agent to achieve convergence-guaranteed signal refinement under geopolitical uncertainty.
- The system employs a recursive coordination protocol where agents exchange typed messages to iteratively adjust portfolio weights until the aggregate change falls below a defined threshold.
- Empirical results demonstrate that RMATS provides superior downside protection and lower maximum drawdown compared to traditional MVO and single-agent baselines during geopolitical stress events.

---

[Tool-Call Dependency Structure is Linearly Decodable in LLM Agent Residual Streams](http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.25310)

- Tool-Call Dependency Structure is Linearly Decodable in LLM Agent Residual Streams: introduces a structural probing methodology to decode directed acyclic graphs of tool-call dependencies from the residual streams of LLMs.
- The research demonstrates that tool-call dependency structures are linearly decodable from frozen residual streams of LLMs, with signals emerging in mid-stack layers and persisting through the final layer.
- Through activation patching and counterfactual analysis, the paper provides evidence that these dependency representations propagate through the model's internal state rather than acting as passive readouts or simple positional artifacts.

---

[Memory Architectures for Multi-Turn Text-to-SQL: A Benchmark and Empirical Study](http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.26394)

- ENTERPRISEMEM-BENCH: introduces a multi-turn Text-to-SQL benchmark that evaluates LLMs across five memory conditions, including Stateless, Working Memory, Episodic Memory, Semantic Memory, and a Context Resolution Layer.
- The framework utilizes a condition-aware SQL agent that performs parallel context resolution and retrieval to address the universal collapse of stateless LLMs in multi-turn database interactions.
- The study demonstrates that while working memory is mandatory for multi-turn performance, additional episodic and semantic memory components provide non-monotonic returns that are highly dependent on the specific model and dataset.

---

[RICE-PO: Turning Retrieval Interactions into Credit Signals for Reasoning Agents](http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.26352)

- RICE-PO: introduces a critic-free policy optimization framework that converts multi-turn retrieval interactions into localized learning signals for reasoning agents.
- The framework utilizes uncertainty-guided anchor selection to identify critical reasoning steps and employs counterfactual branching to estimate the influence and residual stability of these steps on retrieval outcomes.
- By gating the propagation of summary-level retrieval rewards to latent reasoning spans, RICE-PO enables fine-grained credit assignment without requiring learned critics or external process reward models.

---

[MechRL: Reinforcement Learning Agents Perform Circuit Discovery for Mechanistic Interpretability](http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.26343)

- MechRL: introduces a reinforcement learning approach to automate circuit discovery in LLMs by treating attention head ablation as a Markov decision process.
- The framework utilizes a PPO Agent to identify causally indispensable attention heads by maximizing a Contrastive Reward Function that isolates task-specific performance from general model degradation.
- By employing a Best-of-K Planner, the system achieves high-performance transfer to unseen tasks, demonstrating that learned structural priors effectively identify single-head bottlenecks in transformer circuits.

---

[ScientistOne: Towards Human-Level Autonomous Research via Chain-of-Evidence](http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.26340)

- ScientistOne: introduces an end-to-end autonomous research system designed to maintain evidence chains by construction throughout literature review, solution discovery, and paper writing.
- The framework utilizes a Chain-of-Evidence (CoE) standard to ensure every claim is traceable to a grounding source, supported by a CoE Integrity Audit that performs score verification, specification violation detection, reference verification, and method-code alignment.
- ScientistOne achieves superior verifiability compared to baseline systems, demonstrating that maintaining evidence chains at claim-production time is essential for producing trustworthy autonomous research outputs.

---

[JobBench: Aligning Agent Work With Human Will](http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.26329)

- JobBench: introduces a human-centered benchmark for evaluating AI agents on professional workflows that experts prefer to delegate, utilizing an Agentic Workspace, Reference Files, Reasoning Challenges, Fact-anchored Chained Rubrics, Agentic Scaffolds, and an LLM-as-a-judge.
- The framework evaluates LLMs across 130 tasks spanning 35 occupations, grounding performance metrics in domain expert delegation preferences rather than purely economic value.
- JobBench employs a rigorous three-stage quality check pipeline, including automated audits and annotator reviews, to ensure that its 4,631 binary criteria are verifiable and challenging for frontier LLMs.

---

[Anchor: Mitigating Artifact Drift in Agent Benchmark Generation](http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.26321)

- Anchor: introduces a task-generation pipeline that mitigates artifact drift by compiling instructions, environment configurations, oracle solutions, and verifiers from a single solved constraint program.
- The framework utilizes a CP-SAT solver to certify optimal solutions for parametric business workflows, ensuring deterministic consistency across all generated task artifacts.
- The authors apply this pipeline to produce ERP-Bench, a verifiable benchmark of 300 long-horizon procurement and manufacturing tasks, demonstrating that frontier LLMs struggle with business-rule adherence despite high constraint satisfaction rates.

---

[E³C: Video Generation with 3D Environmental Memory and Ego-Exo Human Pose Control](http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.26316)

- E³C: introduces a controllable egocentric video generation framework that unifies 3D Environmental Memory with Ego-Exo Human Pose Control to disentangle persistent scene structure from human-driven dynamics.
- The framework utilizes a semi-dense point cloud-based 3D memory augmented with per-point appearance features to maintain geometric consistency under rapid camera motion.
- E³C incorporates an ego pose encoder that converts 3D body joints and wrist poses into persistent cross-attention tokens, enabling robust motion control even when the wearer's body is partially occluded or out-of-view.

---

[Your Agents Are Aging Too: Agent Lifespan Engineering for Deployed Systems](http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.26302)

- AgingBench: introduces a longitudinal benchmark framework for evaluating and diagnosing time-dependent reliability degradation in long-lived LLM agents.
- The framework organizes agent aging into four distinct mechanisms—compression, interference, revision, and maintenance—and utilizes a temporal dependency DAG to enable controlled, reproducible evaluation.
- AgingBench employs a component-aware diagnostic pipeline with counterfactual probes to attribute failures to specific stages, including Write/Compress Policy (W), Memory Store (S), Read/Retrieval Algorithms (R), and Utilization Logic (U).

---

[Sandlock: Confining AI Agent Code with Unprivileged Linux Primitives](http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.26298)

- Sandlock: introduces a split-enforcement architecture for unprivileged AI agent sandboxing that separates static kernel-enforced rules from runtime-dependent supervisor decisions.
- The framework utilizes Landlock (kernel-enforced static filesystem/network/IPC rules), seccomp-bpf (unconditional syscall filtering), seccomp user notification supervisor (async task handling runtime-dependent decisions), policy_fn (programmable runtime policy callback), COW backends (unprivileged filesystem write capture), and pipeline operator (stage-based capability composition) to provide low-latency, programmable confinement.
- Sandlock achieves sub-10 ms startup overhead and near-native performance by minimizing supervisor involvement through the use of static kernel-enforced policies for predictable operations.

---

[Agentic AI Workload Characteristics](http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.26297)

- Agentic AI Workload Characteristics: introduces an end-to-end tracing infrastructure to characterize ReAct-style agent workloads across LLM serving and tool execution perspectives.
- The study reveals that agentic workloads are decode-dominated due to effective context caching, while tool execution latency and failure-driven retries significantly impact end-to-end performance.
- The research demonstrates that agents exhibit a temporal shift from early exploration-heavy tool use to later action-oriented execution, with failed tasks often amplifying system load through repeated retries and increased context growth.

---

[Decoupled Delay Compensation: Enhancing Pre-trained MARL Policies via Learned Dynamics Filtering](http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.26286)

- Decoupled Delay Compensation framework: introduces a modular execution-stage state-estimation layer that replaces delayed observations with current belief-state estimates using a GRU Predictor, a Kalman Filter, a Fusion Module, and a pre-trained MARL Policy.
- The framework functions as a plug-in middleware that enables pre-trained MARL policies to maintain robustness against stochastic communication delays and packet loss without requiring policy retraining.
- By utilizing a learned GRU-based world model and recursive Kalman updates, the system provides synchronized belief representations for action selection, effectively decoupling state estimation from the original policy optimization objective.

---

[Coalition Free Energy and Adaptive Precision in Multi-Agent Cooperation](http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.26278)

- GT-FEP (Game-Theoretic Free Energy Principle): introduces a variational framework that models multi-agent coalition formation through a Gibbs distribution over interacting agents to address credit assignment under uncertainty.
- The framework utilizes APC (Adaptive Precision Control) to dynamically tune sensory precision, ensuring agents operate at an optimal inverted-U peak of credit assignment.
- By employing Harsanyi dividends for synergy-aware credit decomposition, the approach achieves robust coordination and emergent collective order in both real-world traffic datasets and multi-agent control tasks.

---

[SPEAR: Code-Augmented Agentic Prompt Optimization](http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.26275)

- SPEAR (Sandboxed Prompt Engineer with Active Rollback): introduces a free-form agentic optimizer that utilizes an Agent Brain, Python Sandbox, Evaluate Tool, Set Prompt Tool, Finish Tool, AgentState, Auto-rollback Guardrail, and Guard-metric Floor to autonomously improve prompts.
- The framework replaces fixed APE pipelines with an agent that actively authors analysis code over evaluation DataFrames to identify and resolve complex error patterns.
- SPEAR employs metric-based guardrails to ensure monotone improvement, effectively outperforming existing APE methods on industrial judge-prompt tasks by leveraging code-as-action capabilities.

---

[AgentSecBench: Measuring Prompt Injection, Privacy Leakage, and Tool-Use Integrity in LLM Agents](http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.26269)

- AgentSecBench: introduces a formal security framework for LLM agents that measures instruction integrity, retrieval confidentiality, and capability integrity through intent-to-execution noninterference.
- The framework distinguishes between prompt annotation and enforcement by measuring pre-generation channel closure alongside adversarial advantage to evaluate defense effectiveness.
- AgentSecBench utilizes paired adversarial and benign-control executions to isolate security failures from model behavior, providing a rigorous method for auditing LLM agent security boundaries.

---

[Personalizing Embodied Multimodal Large Language Model Agents over Long-term User Interactions](http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.26256)

- POLAR: introduces a multimodal memory-augmented framework that organizes prior interactions into an object-centric knowledge graph to support personalized instance grounding and embodied planning.
- The framework utilizes a Memorization Module to convert raw interaction logs into structured semantic and episodic memories, which are then accessed by a Utilization Module to guide an MLLM agent's navigation decisions.
- By connecting personalized context across multiple episodes, POLAR enables agents to resolve implicit user intent and perform more accurate instance-level navigation compared to standard MLLM-based embodied agents.

---

[Sentinel: Embodied Cooperative Spatial Reasoning and Planning](http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.26239)

- CoSaR: introduces a cooperative spatial reasoning and planning framework that integrates foundation models with classical navigation algorithms to enable decentralized embodied agents to coordinate in dynamic city-scale environments.
- The framework utilizes a structured spatial memory, including occupancy maps and ETA data, to support multi-agent communication, spatial reasoning, and collaborative trajectory replanning.
- CoSaR incorporates a multi-stage route refinement process and emergency avoidance mechanisms to ensure safe navigation while avoiding dynamic hazards modeled as sentinels.

---

[AgentSociety: Incentivizing Agentic Social Intelligence](http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.26203)

- AgentSociety: introduces a decentralized framework for multi-agent systems that leverages social choice theory to incentivize collaboration among self-interested agents through transitive vote delegation and strategic information diffusion.
- The framework models interactions as a Partially Observable Stochastic Game, where agents optimize individual utility by selectively disclosing competence signals to neighbors to influence routing paths.
- AgentSociety enables decentralized multi-task routing in heterogeneous LLM-based multi-agent systems by aligning individual strategic behavior with collective outcomes through fair, marginal-contribution-based payoffs.

---

[CyberEvolver: Structured Self-Evolution for Cybersecurity Agents On the Fly](http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.26195)

- CyberEvolver: introduces a self-evolving cybersecurity agent framework that iteratively revises its own scaffold using Strategy (LS), Environment Interface (LI), Domain Knowledge (LD), and Perception (LP) layers.
- The framework utilizes a Trajectory Diagnosis Pipeline to convert noisy execution logs into actionable revision signals for the Mutation Operator.
- CyberEvolver employs a population-based Beam Search strategy to maintain diverse agent variants and improve success rates across cybersecurity benchmarks.

---

[FuzzPilot: Plateau-Triggered Recipe Validation for Structured Text Fuzzing](http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.26539)

- FuzzPilot: introduces an off-hot-path controller that uses Ghidra headless, Model client, Plateau detector, Blackboard, Agents, and Micro-campaign runner to generate and validate mutation recipes for the AFL++ runner, Corpus, Custom mutator, and Target binary.
- The framework offloads expensive reasoning to an asynchronous controller, ensuring that the AFL++ hot path remains focused on high-throughput mutation using data-as-recipe objects.
- Preliminary evaluation on cJSON demonstrates that while the controller reduces plateau duration, the LLM-based proposal layer requires non-saturated targets to show measurable coverage improvements.

---

[ATOM: Instantiating Budget-Controllable Multi-Agent Collaboration via Nucleus-Electron Hierarchy](http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.26178)

- ATOM: introduces a hierarchical multi-agent framework that decouples a stable Nucleus (stable core reasoning backbone) from dynamic Electrons (dynamically activated peripheral agents) to optimize collaboration topologies based on task complexity.
- The framework utilizes a Complexity Predictor (estimates query difficulty for budgeting) to dynamically regulate agent instantiation, ensuring computational resources align with task demands.
- By employing a task-driven Reinforcement Learning Policy (optimizes task-driven collaboration graphs), ATOM achieves state-of-the-art performance while significantly improving token efficiency compared to static or fully generative multi-agent baselines.

---

[REPOMIRAGE: Probing Repository Context Reasoning in Code Agents with Perturbations](http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.26177)

- REPOMIRAGE: introduces a two-stage evaluation suite that uses semantics-preserving perturbations to probe repository context reasoning in LLMs, with REPOMIRAGE-Perturb, REPOMIRAGE-Extend, REPOANCHOR, and INSTRUCTIONS.md.
- The framework identifies a significant performance gap in LLMs when repository-level structural cues are obscured, revealing that agents often suffer from exploration drift.
- REPOANCHOR mitigates this by separating repository exploration from downstream problem solving, using an intermediate structural summary to anchor agent actions.

---

[A Universal Cliff and a Design Fingerprint: Cross-Section Defect Detection Under LLM Orchestration](http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.26174)

- LLM Orchestration Framework: introduces a study on how partitioning documents across multiple worker agents creates a universal "detection cliff" for cross-section defects that no single agent is positioned to identify.
- The framework utilizes a Controller to decompose tasks and an Integration Step to synthesize reports, revealing that even highly aligned LLMs exhibit an iatrogenic criterion shift where improved detection is coupled with increased false alarms.
- The research demonstrates that at the "bottom of the cliff," LLMs may exhibit anosodiaphoria, where they accurately perceive structural defects in private internal-state probes but fail to weight them as reportable issues in the final integrated output.

---

[ESBMC: A Survey of Its Evolution, Integration, and Future Directions in Formal Software Verification](http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.26169)

- ESBMC: introduces a comprehensive survey of the Efficient SMT-based Context-Bounded Model Checker, detailing its evolution from a research prototype to an industrially capable verification platform integrating Language Front-ends, GOTO program IR, BMC-k-induction encoder, SSA layer, SMT solver portfolio, Verification Engine, and Witness Generator.
- The framework leverages a modular architecture where Language Front-ends translate diverse source code into a common GOTO program IR, which is then processed by the BMC-k-induction encoder and SSA layer before being dispatched to an SMT solver portfolio for formal verification.
- Recent advancements include the integration of LLMs for automated vulnerability repair, loop invariant generation, and agentic model checking, establishing ESBMC as a natively autonomous verification kernel for safety-critical systems.

---

#### 24th May 2026

[Eureka: Intelligent Feature Engineering for Enterprise AI Cloud Resource Demand Prediction](http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.25297)

- Eureka: introduces an agentic framework for automated feature engineering that treats feature creation as an iterative code generation problem to improve predictive performance.
- The framework utilizes an Eureka Expert for planning, an LLM Feature Factory for code generation, and a Self-Evolving Alignment Engine for reinforcement learning-based refinement.
- Eureka integrates domain knowledge and feedback loops to generate executable, domain-aligned features, demonstrating significant improvements in cloud GPU resource demand forecasting.

---

[PR-Aware Automated Unit Test Generation: Challenges and Opportunities](http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.25285)

- PR-Aware Automated Unit Test Generation Framework: evaluates the effectiveness of EvoSuite and GPT-4o in generating fail-to-pass (F2P) unit tests for pull requests by analyzing their ability to capture specific code changes.
- The study utilizes a curated dataset of 353 Java 8 pull requests to compare traditional search-based test generation against single-prompt LLM-based approaches.
- Results indicate that while EvoSuite is more reliable, both tools struggle to generate meaningful tests for pull requests, highlighting the need for feedback-driven, agentic workflows in automated testing.

---

[LLM-as-a-Judge in Healthcare: A Scoping Analysis of Applications, Methods, and Human Alignment](http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.25273)

- LLM-as-a-Judge Framework: introduces a systematic review of LLM-based evaluation methods in healthcare, characterizing how LLMs are configured as judges using Prompt Engineering, Ensemble, RAG, Calibration, Fine-tuning, Multi-agent, and Distillation.
- The paper categorizes healthcare applications into clinical decision support, clinical NLP, medical knowledge and QA, and medical communication, while evaluating judge performance through rubrics and human-alignment metrics.
- The study identifies key failure modes such as evaluation bias from shared model families, conflation of surface presentation with substantive quality, and hallucination, emphasizing the need for rigorous validation and human oversight in clinical deployment.

---

[LipoAgent: Coordinating Fine-Tuned LLM Agents for Safer Lipid Design](http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.25250)

- LipoAgent: introduces a safety-aware multi-agent LLM framework that integrates domain-specific fine-tuning, conditional prediction, and multi-agent verification to optimize lipid discovery for mRNA delivery.
- The framework utilizes a Predictor Agent and a Verifier Agent to jointly model toxicity and transfection efficiency, ensuring that toxicity is treated as a decision-level constraint rather than a post hoc filter.
- By incorporating a human-in-the-loop mechanism for ambiguous cases, LipoAgent achieves high predictive accuracy and reliability while significantly reducing the time and effort required for experimental lipid screening.

---

[FrontierOR: Benchmarking LLMs’ Capacity for Efficient Algorithm Design in Large-Scale Optimization](http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.25246)

- FrontierOR: introduces a benchmark for evaluating LLMs on large-scale optimization algorithm design, utilizing LLM Generation, Evaluation Protocol, Tiny Instances, Feasibility Checker, Baseline Compare, Expert Audit, Agent Repair, Self-evolving Frameworks, and Shared Memory to assess algorithmic efficiency beyond simple formulation.
- The framework evaluates LLMs by requiring them to infer problem structure from natural-language descriptions and generate executable algorithms that compete with expert-verified Gurobi baselines.
- FrontierOR includes 180 literature-grounded tasks and employs self-evolving agents to iteratively refine candidate programs, demonstrating that test-time search significantly improves solution quality and computational efficiency.

---

[Meta-Agent: From Task Descriptions to Verified Multi-Agent Systems](http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.25233)

- Meta-Agent: introduces a two-phase framework that automatically constructs and executes specialized multi-agent systems from natural-language task descriptions.
- The framework utilizes a construction phase to generate a directed acyclic graph of agent specifications and an execution phase that employs continuous verification to prevent cascading errors.
- It incorporates a three-level error attribution mechanism to distinguish between local, upstream, and structural failures, enabling targeted recovery strategies for improved workflow stability.

---

[K8S Power Irrigation: Deep Reinforcement Learning for Performance-Aware Power Efficiency of Kubernetes Cloud-Native Microservices](http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.25218)

- K8SPI (Kubernetes Power Irrigation): introduces a hierarchical reinforcement learning controller that jointly optimizes CPU core and uncore frequencies to minimize power consumption in cloud-native environments while maintaining strict performance requirements.
- The framework utilizes a two-stage control architecture consisting of a Coarse-grained Agent for rapid performance violation mitigation and a Fine-grained Agent for iterative power minimization.
- K8SPI incorporates a multi-level telemetry stack and an offline Config-Action-Observation Snapshot Cache to enable efficient, hardware-in-the-loop reinforcement learning training without live-hardware latency.

---

[GroupTravelBench: Benchmarking LLM Agents on Multi-Person Travel Planning](http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.25200)

- GroupTravelBench: introduces a comprehensive benchmark for multi-user, multi-turn travel planning that evaluates an agent's ability to perform preference elicitation, conflict coordination, and collective planning using Task synthesis pipeline, Multi-party interaction framework, Evaluation protocol, Tool sandbox, User simulators, and LLM judge.
- The framework utilizes a synchronous group-chat environment where one agent interacts with multiple LLM-based user simulators to resolve conflicting preferences and generate feasible itineraries.
- Evaluation is conducted through a combination of rule-based outcome metrics and an LLM-based process assessment to provide a fine-grained analysis of agent performance in real-world group travel scenarios.

---

[Hide to Guide: Learning via Semantic Masking](http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.25198)

- SMEPO (Semantic Masked Expert Policy Optimization): introduces a fine-grained semantic masking strategy for expert-guided RLVR that converts expert traces into fill-in-the-blank guidance by masking reward-relevant spans while preserving procedural scaffolding.
- The framework prevents reward hacking in LLMs by hiding verifier-aligned content—such as final answers, executable code, or critical entities—along the critical path of expert traces.
- SMEPO improves accuracy and convergence efficiency across math, code, and agentic search domains by forcing the policy to reconstruct missing information rather than simply copying exposed expert cues.

---

[DarkForest: Less Talk, Higher Accuracy for Multi-Agent LLMs](http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.25188)

- DarkForest: introduces a controlled-communication coordination framework that aggregates outputs from multiple LLMs by generating candidates independently, constructing a calibrated belief state, and disclosing only compact, policy-permitted evidence to a coordinator.
- The framework utilizes a belief construction mechanism that weights agent support based on historical reliability, parse quality, confidence, and independence corrections to mitigate error propagation and reduce communication overhead.
- A deterministic guardrail provides a final safety check by comparing the coordinator's output against the calibrated belief state, ensuring that strongly supported candidates are not discarded due to single-model errors.

---

[Grow-Prune-Freeze Networks: Adaptive &amp; Continual Learning Technique for Olfactory Navigation](http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.25170)

- GPF (Grow-Prune-Freeze) Networks: introduces an adaptive continual learning framework that dynamically grows, prunes, and freezes neural network layers to maintain stability and plasticity in non-stationary environments.
- The framework utilizes Expected SARSA for policy optimization and grounds its structural adaptation in random matrix theory to ensure spectral stability during layer growth.
- GPF networks demonstrate effective performance in partially observable olfactory navigation tasks and show potential for generalization across reinforcement learning, image classification, and autoregressive LLM tasks.

---

[STREAM: A Data-Centric Framework for Mining High-Value Task-Oriented Dialogues from Streaming Media](http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.25162)

- Stream: introduces a data-centric framework that mines high-value interaction signals from streaming media to synthesize complex task-oriented dialogues using SSI, APS, CB, and IDG.
- The framework leverages RAG-enhanced UUS and ARG to generate realistic, goal-oriented conversations grounded in UPM and APM, while employing DF to ensure dataset quality.
- StreamDial, the resulting large-scale dataset, improves intrinsic dialogue quality and downstream DST performance across multiple domains and languages under controlled training budgets.

---

[SimuWoB: Simulating Real-World Mobile Apps for Fast and Faithful GUI Agent Benchmarking](http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.25160)

- SimuWoB: introduces a fully synthetic benchmark for mobile GUI agents that leverages a Code-generation LLM, PRD Generator, Page Structure Generator, Data Entity Generator, Task-injection Agent, Agent Validator, Manual Check, and Bundle Pool to create high-fidelity, backend-free mobile app environments for efficient evaluation.
- The framework utilizes a two-stage synthesis pipeline where the first stage constructs a minimal working environment and the second stage injects tasks and validators, ensuring both functional completeness and precise reward calculation.
- Comprehensive experiments on state-of-the-art mobile GUI agents reveal significant performance gaps in long-horizon tasks, highlighting the necessity for improved memory, proactive exploration, and fine-grained control in future agent development.

---

[LLM-Agent-Based Renewable Energy Forecasting Using Edge and IoT Data: A Review of Solar, Wind, Weather, and Grid-Aware Decision Support](http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.25141)

- LLM-Agent-Based Renewable Energy Forecasting Framework: introduces a six-layer taxonomy for integrating IoT edge data with LLM agents to enhance renewable energy forecasting through IoT Edge Devices, Data Acquisition Layer, Preprocessing Layer, Feature Engineering Layer, Model Inference Layer, Uncertainty Estimation Layer, and LLM Agent Reasoning and Reporting Layer.
- The framework leverages LLMs to synthesize heterogeneous sensor streams and grid constraints into actionable, human-readable decision support for grid operators.
- This review identifies critical research gaps including decision-aligned forecasting, real-time model adaptation, and the mitigation of LLM hallucinations in safety-critical grid environments.

---

[Trust but Verify: Prover-Verifier Deliberation for Selective LLM Prediction](http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.25133)

- PVD (Prover-Verifier Deliberation): introduces an inference-time protocol for selective prediction where a Prover defends a candidate answer through atomic sub-claims while a Verifier issues targeted challenges to produce an Accept + No Change (ANC) confidence signal.
- The framework utilizes a Prover (LLM generating structured proofs) and a Verifier (LLM issuing targeted challenges) to empirically evaluate argument defensibility rather than relying on simple sample agreement.
- PVD (Prover-Verifier Deliberation): provides a robust diagnostic for LLM reliability by identifying when a Verifier lacks the domain knowledge to sustain adversarial pressure, indicated by the collapse of the ANC accuracy gap.
